# CWE-649: Reliance on Obfuscation without Integrity Checking (mitigated, pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-649-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"crypto/hmac"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"encoding/base64"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"strings"
)

// Safe: HMAC verifies the encoded profile has not been tampered with.
func AdminDashboard(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	raw, err := r.Cookie("profile")
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "unauthorized", http.StatusUnauthorized)
		return
	}
	parts := strings.Split(raw.Value, ".")
	if len(parts) != 2 {
		http.Error(w, "unauthorized", http.StatusUnauthorized)
		return
	}
	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(os.Getenv("COOKIE_KEY")))
	mac.Write([]byte(parts[0]))
	expected := mac.Sum(nil)
	got, _ := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[1])
	if !hmac.Equal(expected, got) {
		http.Error(w, "unauthorized", http.StatusUnauthorized)
		return
	}
	payload, _ := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[0])
	if strings.Contains(string(payload), "role=admin") {
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"panel": "admin"})
		return
	}
	http.Error(w, "forbidden", http.StatusForbidden)
}
